Farn Polrrrcnr- PRACrrcEs CouvrrssroN
428 J Street r Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95g14-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
April 20,2007
Henry T. Perea, Council president
City of Fresno
Fresno City Hall
2600 Fresno Street, 2"d Floor
Fresno, California 93721
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-07-057
Dear Mr. Perea
This letter is_in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act (thel Act').r This letter is ba'sed on the facts
presented. The Fair Political Practices Commission ("Commission,) does not act as a
finder of fact when it renders assistance. (Section s:) t+; In re oglisby (1975) I FppC
Ops.71.)
QUESTION
May you use campaign funds for a politic al and cultural exchange 11.ip to
Germany offered by the Institute for Cultural Diplomacy?
CONCLUSION
You may use campaign funds for expenses incurred on the trip if the expenses are
directly related to a legislative or gor"*m"rtal purpose. Therefore "u*puig" funds may
be used to cover portions of the trip when teglslative or govemmental business is
conducted, as discussed in the analysis. Any expenses iicurred on the trip that are of a
personal nature, however, must be paid for by pirsonal funds, not campai'gn n nas.
FACTS
You are requesting written advice regarding your ability to use campaign funds
for a political and cultural exchange trip to c"*ury offered by the Institute ro? cotto.ut
I Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2,sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
RE
g4


File No. A-07-057
Page No. 2
Diplomacy. You have long believed that interacting with individuals from other
countries, states, and cities can only lead to better understanding of our political and
cultural beliefs.
You provide a description of the trip set forth below. The ..Europe as a Global
Player" study tour is a joint prograrndirected at university or colleg" rrrploy""s, political
officials and companyrepresentatives who want to get a hands-on t-oot ui Germany and
Europe. The aim of the European Host Program is to Urrita strong working relationships
between academics and professionals from North America and Germany tf,rough
international and intercuhural exchange.
The Seminar Program addresses aspects of culfure, economy, politics, and higher
education. Seminar participants will attend lectures grven by speciaiirt. fro- various
universities and experts in the fields of educatio.r, politi.. urrd ""orro-y. The program
takes US and Canadian participants through four different cities, each one ofwhich will
provide thern with a better understanding of a particular aspect of German and European
culture, politics, and life. The Program takes participants to visit the European Central
bank in Frankfurt and the European Parliament in Strasbourg. The progru- visits Berlin
to enable participants to take a closer look at the German perspective *a it, skuggle for
necessary change.
The European Host Program is coordinated jointly by the Ministry of Education,
Women, Youth and Culture Rheinland-Palatinatg and the institute for crliturat
diplomacy. As Rheinland-Palatinate is also home to manymulti-national companies,
including pharmaceutical firms, chernical companies, and construction firms, the state,s
relationship to other nations is very important; hence, the desire to strength* tt "
transatlantic partnership through cultural and academic exchang" tfno"6 piograms tike
the European Host Program.
The European Host Program represents the Institute for Cultural Diplomacy,s
continuing efforts to bring together leaders of civil society in a forum that promotes open
dialogue and cultural exchange. Through internationally-focused programs like this, the
Institute for Cultural Diplomacy hopes to build a sustainabte transatlaitic network and
lay strong foundations for future business and acadernic cooperation between the United
Sates, Canada and Germany. As financial and business relations between these three
countries increase, it becomes more and more important to exchange cultural and social
values.
ANALYSIS
The PoliticalReform Act's provisions concerning the permissible uses of
campaign funds are found in Sections 89510-89522, copiesenctosed. Under these
provi-sions, campaign funds are regarded as totally separate and distinct from a candidate
or officeholder's personal funds. (Kaufinan Advice Letter,No. 4-04-055.) The Act,s
campaign funds provisions are designed to ensure that campaign funds arqused for


File No. A-07-057
Page No. 3
electioneering and officeholder pu{poses, and arenot expended for the candidate or
offi ceholder's personal purposes. (Koufman, supr a.)
The Act provides that all contributions deposited into a campaignaccount are
"held in trust for expenses associated with the election of the candidate"or for expenses
associated with holding office." (Section 89510.) Generally speaking, an expenditure
related to seeking or holding office is permissible under the Act if it if ..reasonably
related" to a political,legislative or govemmental purpose. (Sectio n g9512.) Certain
uses of campaign funds, including travel, that are Considered to confer a ..suLstantial
personal benefrt"'on the candidate must meet a higher standard and be ..directly related
to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose.;, (Section gg5l2.)
With respect to the use of campaign funds for travel, Section 8g513 states:
"(aXl) campaign funds shall not be used to pay or reimburse the
candidate, the elected officer, or any individual or individuals with
authority to approve the expenditure of campaign funds held by a
committee, or anployees or staff of the committee or the elected officer,s
governmental agency for travel expenses and necessary accommodations
except when these expenditures are directly related to a political,
legislative, or govemmental purpose.
"(2) For the purposes of this section, payments or reimbursements for
travel and necessary accommodations shall be considered as directly
related to a political, legislative, or govenrmental purpose if the paynents
would meet standards similar to the standards of the Internal Revenue
Service pursuant to Sections 162 and274 of the Internal Revenue Code for
deductions of travel expenses under the federal income tax law.,,
(Emphasis added.)
Internal Revenue Code Section 162(a)Q) sets forth general rules which permit the
deduction of all ordinary and necessary expenses while traveling away from home in the
pursuit of a trade or business.' Under the Act, these rules have been applied to permit the
use of campaign funds for only the portions of a trip when political, legislative, or
governmental 'obusiness" is conducted. To the extent that any day is nit a business day
Subsfantial personal benefit is defined as "an expenditure of campaigrr funds which results in a
direct personal benefit of more than fwo hundred dollars t$zoo; to a candidatellected of6cer, or any
individual or individual with authority to approve the expfnditure of campaign funds held by a committee.,,
(Section 8951l(bX3).)
In pertinent part, Internar Revenue code Section r6z(a)states that;
"(a) In general- There shall be allowed as a deduction all the oJiou. and necessary expenses paid or
incurred during the taxable year in canyrngon any trade or business, including -
(1) a reasonable allowance for salaries or othei compensation for personal Jervices actually rendered;
(2) traveling expenses (including amountsfor meali ard lodging)ther than amounts which are lavish or
extravagant under the circumstance) while away from home in the pursuit of a tade or business . . ..,,
(Emphasis added.)


File No. A-07-057
Page No. 4
(such as days on which there are no rneetings or formal activities), campaignfunds may
not be used. If necessary to prorate the cost, your trip to German y ma; beiroken into
business days and nonbusiness days. When a weekend falls betrveen blsiness days, the
weekend will also be counted as business time. (BassAdvice Letter,No. 4-90-474.)
Moreover, expenditures for solely personal purposes, such as some of the meals,
entertainment, guided 9yl,tural and sight-seeing activities, may not be paid for with
campaign funds, even if those occur on a business day. (Hosterman Adviceletter, No.
A-05-044.) To the extent these personal expens"r uri part of a package price for the trip,
they must be subtracted out.
If you have other questions on this matter,please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
t/^/" f Nay,,r,,-z
By: Hyla P. Wagner
Senior Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosures
Hwjgl
